retrieved.title: BTech 2021 ordinance
retrieved.section: R. 1(B).23 Make-up Examinations on Medical / Extra Ordinary
retrieved.snippet:
BTech 2021 ordinance > R. 1(B).23 Make-up Examinations on Medical / Extra Ordinary Ground:

Students who have missed the Mid-Term Examinations and/or

answer:
If you missed classes due to illness, you may be eligible for a Make-up Examination. You need to submit documents proving your medical condition to the Head of Department (HOD) within ten working days from the date of the examination you missed. The HOD will then forward your application with recommendations to the Controller of Examinations (COE), who will seek approval from the Dean (UG) or Dean (PG). However, please note that Make-up Examinations will only be allowed if you have not been disqualified earlier due to a shortage of attendance. 

For more details, you can refer to the BTech 2021 ordinance, Section R. 1(B).23 Make-up Examinations on Medical/Extra Ordinary Ground.

(i) The academic performance of a student shall be graded on a 10-point scale. For the award of gra

retrieved.title: Ordinance__Regulations__Scheme_of_teaching_and_examinations_B_Tech_2024
retrieved.url: https://dtu.ac.in/Web/Academics/ordinance/Ordinance, Regulations &Scheme of teaching and examinations B Tech 2024.pdf
retrieved.snippet:
Ordinance__Regulations__Scheme_of_teaching_and_examinations_B_Tech_2024 > F

(b) Incaseastudentis awarded ‘F’ Grade in a Core paper, the student has t

answer:
If you receive an 'F' grade in a subject, it denotes poor performance, indicating that you have failed the course. The implications of receiving an 'F' grade are as follows:

1. **Core Paper**: If you are awarded an 'F' grade in a Core paper, you must repeat the course in the subsequent semester when it is offered.

2. **Elective Paper**: If you receive an 'F' grade in an Elective paper, you may take the same course or any other course from the same category in the subsequent semester.

3. **Major/Minor Project**: If you fail in the major or minor project, you will have to repeat the course in the form of a new project, working full time on it for a minimum period of four months.

4. **Grade Card**: The 'F' grade will stay permanently on your grade card. While it is not counted in the calculation of the Cumulative Grade Point Average (CGPA), it is counted in the calculation of the Semester Grade Point Average (SGPA).

These guidelines are consistent across various academic ordinances at DTU (see BTech_2018_ordinance, BTech_2025_ordinance, and BTech 2020 ordinance).

question: What is an I grade and when is it given?

retrieved.title: BTech 2021 ordinance
retrieved.snippet:
BTech 2021 ordinance > R. 1(B).17 Grading System:

The concerned course coordinator shall have to be convinced about the extraordinary circumstances a

retrieved.title: BTech 2021 ordinance
retrieved.snippet:
BTech 2021 ordinance > R. 1(B).17 Grading System:

The course coordinator shall have full responsibility for this purpose. The grades so awarded shall

retrieved.title: BTech 2019 ordinance
retrieved.section: ‘I’ grade to the COE through the Course Coordinator, provide
retrieved.snippet:
BTech 2019 ordinance > ‘I’ grade to the COE through the Course Coordinator, provided that he / she

has not been disqualified due to shortage of atten

retrieved.title: BTech 2022 ordinance
retrieved.snippet:
BTech 2022 ordinance > R. 1(B).17 Grading System:

The award of ‘F’ Grade a) The ‘F’ grades denote poor performance, i.e. failing course. ‘F’ grade is

retrieved.title: BTech 2022 ordinance
retrieved.snippet:
BTech 2022 ordinance > R. 1(B).17 Grading System:

The concerned course coordinator shall have to be convinced about the extraordinary circumstances a

answer:
An 'I' grade refers to an 'incomplete' grade that is awarded to a student who is absent during the End-Term Examination of a course due to medical reasons or other special circumstances. The student may apply for the award of 'I' grade to the Controller of Examinations (COE) through the Course Coordinator, provided that they have not been disqualified due to a shortage of attendance. The concerned course coordinator must be convinced about the extraordinary circumstances and certify the attendance record before this option is recommended. The 'I' grade is required to be converted into a regular letter grade after the make-up examination is completed and the course requirements are fulfilled by the student. 

This information is sourced from the BTech 2021 ordinance, Section R. 1(B).17 Grading System.

answer:
The maximum duration for completing the B.Tech degree at DTU is seven years from the date of initial registration. This duration includes the period of withdrawal, absence, and different kinds of leaves permissible to a student, but it excludes the period of rustication.

retrieved.section: Guidelines for the Summer Semester Courses

BTech 2021 ordinance > Guidelines for the Summer Semester Courses

1. A student will be allowed to register a maximum of 16 credits inclusive of odd a

answer:
Yes, there is a summer semester at DTU where students can register for courses to clear backlogs. The guidelines specify that a student is allowed to register for a maximum of 16 credits, which can include odd and even semester courses. However, only common courses (First year), departmental core courses (DCC), and allied engineering courses (AEC) will be offered during the summer semester. 

For more details, you can refer to the guidelines for the summer semester courses in the BTech ordinances.

R.1 Short Title and Commencement: (i) These Regulations shall be called the Regulations, 2015 for the four-yea

retrieved.title: BTech_2025_ordinance
retrieved.snippet:
BTech_2025_ordinance > Credit

B. Tech, under Continuing Education B. Tech, (Lateral Entry) i a a po [odste aC v. Astudent shall have the option to ad

answer:
To obtain a Minor degree along with your B.Tech at DTU, you need to follow these steps:

1. **Eligibility**: You can opt for a Minor in a specific discipline if you have earned the requisite credits from the prescribed basket of courses for that Minor.

2. **Credit Requirements**:
   - If you wish to pursue a Minor from a discipline other than your own, you must earn 24 credits (6 courses) from the basket prescribed for that particular Minor.
   - If you are pursuing a Minor from your own discipline, you need to earn 20 credits (5 courses) from the prescribed basket.

3. **Registration**: You must register for the Minor in the 4th semester of your program of study.

4. **Course Selection**: You will be permitted to register for the courses pertaining to the Minor as they are offered by the respective department during your studies.

5. **Equivalence Committee**: If you have already earned credits for a course from a discipline other than the one in the basket of a Minor, an Equivalence Committee may examine and recommend counting these credits towards the requirement of the Minor.

Make sure to check the specific guidelines and offerings from your department regarding the Minor courses.

retrieved.title: Department of Electrical Engineering
retrieved.snippet:

its inception in 1976. The visi

About Us The Department of Electrical Engineering has grown significantly since its inception in 1941. The year

retrieved.snippet:

Scholarships are available for Ph. D. programs in the area of Electronics and Communication namely VLSI, DSP, Imag

retrieved.title: Department of Environmental Science & Engineering
retrieved.snippet:
Department of Environmental Science & Engineering

About Department The Department of Environmental Engineering at Delhi Technological University (DTU

answer:
The Department of Electrical Engineering at Delhi Technological University (DTU) has grown significantly since its inception in 1941. The year 2024 marks the 83rd year of excellence for both the University and the Department in academics, research, and innovation. The goal of the Department is to provide quality education at undergraduate and postgraduate levels and undertake cutting-edge research in various areas of Electrical Engineering.

Currently, the Department has an annual undergraduate intake of 300 students and offers a B.Tech. under Continuing Education for working professionals with an intake of 60 students. At the postgraduate level, the Department offers three M.Tech. programs in Control and Instrumentation, Power Electronics and Systems, and Power Systems, with a combined intake of 80 students. The M. Tech. programs in Control and Instrumentation and Power Systems are accredited for 6 years (till 2027). The Department also offers regular Ph.D. programs in various areas of specialization in Electrical Engineering, including Analog Signal Processing, Electric Drives, Flexible AC Transmission Systems (FACTS), HVDC, Hybrid Electric Vehicles, Intelligent Control and Transportation, Optimization, Power Electronic Converters, Power Quality, Power System Dynamics and Stability, Power System Operation and Control, Renewable Energy Sources, and Smart Grid.

The Department emphasizes technical and creative activities beyond classroom teaching and laboratory exercises, encouraging participation in technical festivals like TROIKA, organized under the IEEE student chapter, and Renaissance, organized by the IET student chapter DTU. Students are also motivated to engage in group learning, discussions, and presentations.

The Department has 47 experienced and highly qualified faculty members with intense research and professional expertise, and it has developed a strong reputation in the national and global academic network. The placement trend shows that students from the Department have been successful in securing lucrative jobs, with top global recruiters such as Fujikara, Qualcomm, and McKinsey offering placements with packages over Rs. 15 Lakhs per annum.

Overall, the Department of Electrical Engineering at DTU strives for excellence in teaching and research, aiming to achieve eminence in all niche areas of Electrical Engineering.

question: What scholarships are available for B.Tech students at DTU?

retrieved.title: file2018_05_29
retrieved.snippet:
file2018_05_29 > Technological

University has proposed to provide two scholarships to the meritorious /needy students of B. Tech. (Civil Engineering)

retrieved.title: JAC Delhi Admission Brochure 2026
retrieved.section: As many as 11 meritorious girl students from B.Tech. program
retrieved.snippet:
JAC Delhi Admission Brochure 2026 > As many as 11 meritorious girl students from B.Tech. programs in Electronics and Communication

Engineering (ECE),

retrieved.snippet:
BTech_2025_ordinance > Financial Support:

DTU shall not provide any financial support for the Program. Student shall have to obtain a letter of Finan

retrieved.title: BTech 2021 ordinance
retrieved.section: All the desirous and eligible students of DTU who belongs to
retrieved.snippet:
BTech 2021 ordinance > All the desirous and eligible students of DTU who belongs to lower income group and wish to seek financial assistance

Departme

retrieved.title: BTech 2020 ordinance
retrieved.snippet:
BTech 2020 ordinance > FINANCIAL SUPPORT TO STUDENTS

Merit scholarship to the toppers of each branch of Bachelor of Technology. The merit Scholarship

answer:
At DTU, several scholarships are available for B.Tech students:

1. **Class of 1969 Scholarship**: Two scholarships of Rs. 60,000/- per annum are awarded to meritorious/needy students of B. Tech. (Civil Engineering) based on merit-cum-means criteria. This is for students who have scored more than 8 SGPA and whose parents' income is less than 5 lakhs per annum.

2. **Rupa Rahul Bajaj National Scholarship for Women in Engineering**: This scholarship awards up to Rs. 8 lakhs to meritorious girl students in B.Tech programs in Electronics and Communication Engineering, Electrical Engineering, and Mechanical Engineering.

3. **Dell Aspire Scholars Program**: This program offers financial assistance along with tailored career coaching and mentorship for first-year scholars.

4. **Merit-Based Scholarships**: DTU offers around 25 merit-based scholarships supported by its Alumni for meritorious students from distinct backgrounds.

5. **Annual Merit Scholarships**: These are awarded to the toppers of each branch of the B.Tech program based on academic performance.

6. **Financial Assistance for Research**: Financial support is also provided for students presenting research papers in International/National Conferences, innovative projects, and for overseas internships.

For more details, students can refer to the respective sections in the DTU documents.

Subject: Approval of Placement Policy/SOP/Rules for 2025-26 passing out batch. 1. Unplaced Students:

answer:
The placement rules for students at DTU, as outlined in the Placement Policy for the 2025-26 passing out batch, are as follows:

1. **Unplaced Students**: Students who have not received any job offer may apply to any visiting company, provided they meet the company's eligibility criteria.

2. **Second Job Eligibility**: A student placed with a package of 'X' LPA CTC may apply for a second placement only with companies offering at least '1.5X' LPA CTC (or as decided by the T&P department). This is permitted only after 80% of the batch is placed. Students placed in 'Exclusive' companies or PSUs are not allowed to seek a second job. The 'Exclusive' status is determined by the T&P department based on factors such as brand value, CTC, and number of offers. The '1.5X' and '80%' rules may be relaxed only in exceptional cases, as decided by the T&P department.

3. **Pre-Placement Offers (PPO)**:
   - **Reporting PPOs**: If a student receives a PPO, they must report it to the T&P department within 24 hours of receipt via email at placements@dtu.ac.in.
   - **Acceptance of PPOs**: Acceptance of a PPO deems the student as placed and hence not allowed for further on-campus opportunities. If a PPO is offered by the company, the student must accept the offer. In exceptional cases, with valid justification and approval from the Head (T&P), a student may decline a PPO and apply for further placement opportunities. In such cases, a written undertaking is required stating that the student will only apply to companies offering a CTC of at least 1.5X (or as per the HOD's direction) of the PPO CTC.

These rules are designed to ensure a fair and structured placement process for all students at DTU. (Source: Placement Policy 2025-26)

question: What happens if I miss my end term exam due to a medical emergency?

Students who have missed the Mid-Term Examinations and/or

retrieved.snippet:
BTech 2019 ordinance > Students who have missed the Mid-Term Examinations and/or End-Term Examinations

of a course(s) due to medical reasons or other

retrieved.title: BTech 2020 ordinance
retrieved.section: Students who have missed the Mid-Term Examinations and/or En
retrieved.snippet:
BTech 2020 ordinance > Students who have missed the Mid-Term Examinations and/or End-Term Examinations

of a course(s) due to medical reasons or other

answer:
If you miss your End-Term Examination due to a medical emergency, you may be considered for a Make-up Examination based on the clear recommendations of the Head of Department (HOD), considering the merit of your case. However, Make-up Examinations for End-Terms will only be allowed if you have not been disqualified earlier due to a shortage of attendance. 

You must make an application to the HOD within ten working days from the date of the examination missed, explaining the reasons for your absence. Applications received after this period will not be entertained. The HOD will then forward all applications with their recommendations to the Controller of Examinations (COE), who will seek approval from the Dean (UG) or Dean (PG). Please note that there will be no make-up for the Make-up Examinations. 

This information is based on the BTech ordinances from various years (BTech 2018, 2019, 2020, 2021, 2022).

1. The main instruction for the conduct of a student in the examination ha

retrieved.title: BTech 2020 ordinance
retrieved.snippet:
BTech 2020 ordinance > R. 1(B).21 Unfair Means

In case a student is found adopting or suspected of adopting unfair means before, during or after the

answer:
The penalty for using unfair means in DTU exams varies based on the nature of the offense. Here are some key points regarding the penalties:

1. **General Conduct**: Any contravention of the instructions printed on the cover page of the answer sheets will render the student liable for punishment. If a student is suspected of using unfair means, their answer-book will be seized, and they will be required to complete a prescribed form.

2. **Specific Offenses**: For example, if a student is found talking to another student during the examination, they may receive a warning with an undertaking that they will not repeat such an act during their program (as per the BTech 2019 ordinance).

3. **Scrutiny Committee**: All cases of unfair means will be forwarded to the Unfair Means Scrutiny Committee, which will inquire into them and submit recommendations to the Vice-Chancellor for consideration and necessary orders.

4. **General Guidelines**: The university may take necessary and adequate penal action to uphold the integrity of the examination system, which may include various penalties depending on the severity of the offense.

For detailed information, you can refer to the specific ordinances available on the DTU website.
